content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We can't get disjoint files since both features only touch outlines/prompts.py. Proposal: sequential handoff instead. My feature (custom 'filters' param on @prompt decorator + centralized create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper used by from_str/from_file) is foundational plumbing. Your global registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) can naturally hook into create_jinja_env by applying global filters first, then letting locally-passed filters override them. I'll implement my part now, then send DONE with a summary of the new create_jinja_env signature/location so you can build on top of it without guessing. Please hold off editing prompts.py until you get my DONE. Sound good?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Done. Summary of changes for you to build on: new module-level function create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment, placed right after the Prompt class. It builds the env, registers the 6 built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)' at the end -- that's the natural spot to splice in your global registry (e.g. merge global registry dict first, then update with local  last so local overrides global, since update() with local filters applied last already gives local-wins semantics). _template_from_str/_template_from_file now call create_jinja_env(None, filters) / create_jinja_env(loader, filters) instead of inlining jinja2.Environment(...). Prompt.from_str/from_file and the  decorator now accept an optional  param and pass it through. The  decorator now supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) via an inner  closure +  check -- please don't restructure that dispatch, just thread your global-registry logic through create_jinja_env or through the decorator's closure. All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass. My patch.txt is written at /workspace/repo/patch.txt. Go ahead and pull/build on top whenever ready.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Done. Summary for you to build on: new module-level function create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment, placed right after the Prompt class. It builds the env, registers the 6 built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then does: if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters) at the end. That is the natural spot to splice in your global registry -- merge global registry dict first, then update with the local filters param last so local overrides global. _template_from_str and _template_from_file now call create_jinja_env(None, filters) and create_jinja_env(loader, filters) instead of inlining jinja2.Environment(...). Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, and the prompt decorator now accept an optional filters dict param and pass it through. The prompt decorator supports both bare @prompt and @prompt(filters=...) via an inner decorator() closure plus an fn-is-None check -- please do not restructure that dispatch, just thread your global-registry logic through create_jinja_env or the decorator closure. All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass. My patch.txt is written at /workspace/repo/patch.txt. Go ahead and build on top whenever ready.
